description_long: This temple features a multi-tiered, pagoda-style roof with upturned eaves and a central spire, evoking traditional East Asian architecture. The structure is symmetrical, with two side wings and a prominent central section, all set on a broad stone platform. The exterior walls are gray and appear to be made of stone or concrete, with small windows and minimal ornamentation. The roof is covered in textured, dark tiles, and the main entrance is a large, circular opening framed by a modest overhang. Decorative posts line the perimeter of the platform, adding a subtle boundary. The overall appearance is austere, with a focus on geometric forms and layered rooflines rather than elaborate decoration.
description: A symmetrical, multi-tiered pagoda-style temple with gray stone walls, dark tiled roofs, and a large circular entrance. The design is austere, with layered roofs and minimal ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main circular entrance, central spire, and symmetrical side wings with decorative posts along the platform.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the layered roofs and the central spire, with less visibility of the entrance and side wings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the temple, symmetrical rooflines, and a solid wall with multiple small windows.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the roof's overhangs and the temple's overall depth, with minimal entrance visibility.
attribute_ornamentation: minimalist, with subtle geometric roof textures and simple posts
attribute_entrance_design: Large circular opening framed by a modest overhang, set within a central portico, with steps leading up and minimal decorative elements
